long_stay: Preparatory courses linked to a higher education program (max 1 year)
qualifying_courses.notes: Must be full-time study at a publicly accredited institution. Distance learning, part-time studies, or courses at unaccredited private institutions do not qualify. From May 2, 2025, students in non-state-approved programs face restrictions on work permits, post-study stays, and family visas.

types.name: Student Residence Permit (ST1)
types.notes: Serves as both visa and residence permit for non-EU/EEA or Swiss citizens.

booking_methods.description: For submitting biometric data and original documents.
Partner external outsourcing hub for submitting biometric data and original documents.

steps.title: Get Accepted
steps.description: Receive an unconditional letter of admission from a state-approved Danish higher education institution.

steps.title: Institution Initiates Application
steps.description: The educational institution completes its section (Part 2) of the online ST1 form and provides a unique reference ID (Case Order ID).

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the mandatory processing fee to the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI).

steps.title: Complete Student's Section
steps.description: Fill out your section (Part 1) of the online ST1 application, linking it to your Case Order ID, and attach required documents.

steps.title: Submit Biometrics
steps.description: Visit a Danish Embassy, Consulate General, or VFS Global station within 14 days of submitting your online application to record biometric data (fingerprints and facial photograph) and present original physical documents.
steps.action_required: Book appointment immediately after online submission.

steps.title: Wait for Processing
steps.description: SIRI typically processes complete applications within 60 days, though it can take up to 3 months in practice.

steps.title: Collect Permit and Travel
steps.description: Collect your residence permit and travel to Denmark.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Valid for the entire duration of studies; permit cannot extend beyond 3 months before passport expiry.

required_documents.name: Completed ST1 Application Form
required_documents.details: Completed online; educational institution completes the first part, student completes the second.

required_documents.name: Proof of Financial Means
required_documents.details: Personal certified bank statement in applicant's name, less than 30 days old, showing the balance and currency. Statements under a parent's name or cryptocurrency are not accepted.
exemptions: Students who have paid tuition for their program
PhD students with a job contract

required_documents.name: Proof of English or Danish Language Proficiency
Fee Payment Receipt
required_documents.details: Proof of payment for the SIRI application fee.

required_documents.name: Proof of Tuition Payment
required_documents.details: Documentation proving the first semester or year's tuition was paid (if applicable, for full-degree non-EU students). This can exempt from financial proof.

required_documents.name: Application Confirmation Receipt
required_documents.details: Printout of the completed online ST1 application confirmation page.

required_documents.name: Declaration of Consent (for minors)
required_documents.details: From both parents and documentation of custody holders (for applicants under 18).

acceptable_proof: Personal certified bank statements in applicant's name (less than 30 days old, showing balance and currency)
Official scholarship award letter specifying monthly stipends
Blocked account in applicant's name or Danish account (if sponsored by third-party)
exemptions: Students who have paid tuition for their program
PhD students with a job contract

residence_permit.authority_full_name: Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration
residence_permit.booking_method: Visit local Citizen Service center (Borgerservice) or International Citizen Service (ICS) hub
residence_permit.notes: The student residence permit (ST1) is applied for before arrival and serves as both visa and residence permit. Upon arrival, students must register their address with the local municipality (Borgerservice/ICS) within 5 days to obtain a CPR number (Civil Registration Number) and access public services, including free medical care. The permit is valid for the nominal duration of the educational program.

restrictions: Work limit is 90 hours per month during the normal study period (September-May).
Full-time work is allowed during June, July, and August.
Applies to students in state-approved higher educational programs.
Students in non-state-approved programs (applied on/after May 2, 2025) are generally not granted work permits.
Basic/youth program students are generally not permitted to work.
Unpaid voluntary work is unlimited and does not count toward the limit.
work_while_studying.notes: Exceeding work limits can lead to warning, fine, or permit revocation and deportation.

required_documents: Statement from educational institution confirming active enrollment and student status
Updated bank records proving financial self-sufficiency for the remaining duration
Proof of sufficient funds (DKK 7,426/month) for the remaining period (if permit granted for less than 1 year)
Proof of paid tuition (if applicable, exempts from financial documentation)
permit_renewal.notes: Must not be delayed by more than 1 year in studies.

requirements: Completion of a Danish professional bachelor, bachelor, master or PhD educational programme (state-approved).
post_study_work.notes: After completing a state-approved higher education program, students are generally granted a job-seeking permit of 6 months or 3 years, depending on the program type. PhD students typically receive a 3-year permit. During the job-seeking period, work rights are the same as during studies (90 hours/month Sept-May, full-time June-Aug) unless a full work permit is obtained. Non-state-approved programs (applied on/after May 2, 2025) are not eligible for a job-seeking permit. If a job is secured, students can start working while their work permit application is processed.

official_sources.name: New to Denmark (SIRI – official portal)
official_sources.description: The official joint portal of the Danish Immigration Service and SIRI, serving as the definitive legal source for all residence permit tracks.

official_sources.name: Study in Denmark (official guide)
official_sources.description: The state-run hub for academic structures, program accreditations, and student life information.

official_sources.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark
official_sources.description: For consular fees, embassy processing locations, and diplomatic appointment requirements.

tips.title: Apply in Advance
tips.body: It is highly recommended to apply for your student residence permit 3–4 months before your program start date, as processing can take up to 3 months.

tips.title: Verify Program Accreditation
tips.body: Always verify program accreditation status before applying, especially due to new 2025 rules affecting work and post-study rights for non-approved programs.

tips.title: Financial Documentation Validity
tips.body: Ensure bank statements for financial documentation are less than 30 days old at the time of submission, in your own name, and clearly state the currency. Parent statements or cryptocurrency are not accepted.

tips.title: Biometrics Submission
tips.body: After submitting your online application, book your embassy/VFS appointment immediately to submit biometrics (fingerprints and photo) within the mandatory 14-day window.

tips.title: Understand Work Limits
tips.body: Strictly track your working hours. The current rule is 90 hours per month during the normal study period (September-May) and full-time during June, July, and August. Exceeding these limits can lead to permit revocation and deportation.

tips.title: Timely Permit Renewal
tips.body: Apply for your residence permit extension no earlier than 4 months and no later than the day your current permit expires.

tips.title: Register CPR Address
tips.body: Upon arrival, register your address with the local municipality (Borgerservice/ICS) immediately to obtain your CPR number, which is essential for accessing healthcare, bank accounts, and other services.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying too close to the program start date
common_mistakes.consequence: Potential delays or rejection of the application.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply 3-4 months in advance, as processing can take up to 3 months.

common_mistakes.mistake: Enrolling in a non-state-approved program without knowing the restrictions
common_mistakes.consequence: Loss of work and post-study rights (for applications on/after May 2, 2025).
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Always verify program accreditation status before applying.

common_mistakes.mistake: Submitting bank statements older than 30 days
common_mistakes.consequence: Application rejection due to invalid financial proof.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure bank statements are less than 30 days old at the time of submission.

common_mistakes.mistake: Forgetting to submit biometrics within 14 days of online application
common_mistakes.consequence: Application rejected as invalid, and loss of application fee.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Book your embassy/VFS appointment immediately after submitting online.

common_mistakes.mistake: Exceeding the monthly work limit for students
common_mistakes.consequence: Warning, fine, or immediate revocation of residence permit and deportation.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Track your hours carefully, adhering to the 90 hours per month limit during term time (September-May) and full-time during summer breaks (June-August).

common_mistakes.consequence: Risk of overstaying and legal issues.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply for extension no earlier than 4 months and no later than the day your permit expires.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not registering CPR address upon arrival
common_mistakes.consequence: Inability to obtain a CPR number, access healthcare, bank accounts, and other essential services.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Register with the local municipality immediately after moving in.

common_mistakes.mistake: Renting without an address registration right
common_mistakes.consequence: Inability to obtain a CPR number, rendering your stay functionally non-compliant.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Always verify that the landlord permits you to register your CPR address at the apartment.
